title: Split a Fat Commit
description: A colleague pushed one huge commit that mixes a bug fix, a new feature, and a config change — three unrelated concerns. You need to split it into three separate, well-named commits without losing any of the changes.
objective: The history must contain exactly three commits in place of the original fat commit: one for the bug fix (`bugfix.js`), one for the feature (`feature.js`), and one for the config change (`config.json`). Each commit must touch only its own file. All content must be preserved.
hints: Use `git rebase -i HEAD~1` to get to the fat commit. Change `pick` to `edit`.
Git will pause at that commit. Use `git reset HEAD~` to unstage all changes back to the working tree.
Then `git add -p` or `git add <specific-file>` to stage and commit each concern separately.
